item.title: Restore temp directory created in /tmp without mode 0700 — decrypted backup contents world-readable during restore
item.intent: Decrypted backup contents MUST NOT be readable by other users during restore — the temp directory in /tmp must be created with mode 0700 before any decrypted data lands in it.
item.description: src/operate/backup/restore.ts line 151 calls mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true }) where tempDir = join(tmpdir(), 'clawhq-restore-{timestamp}'). This creates the temp directory in /tmp with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. The restore pipeline then decrypts the encrypted backup archive INTO this temp directory (line 174: decryptedPath = join(tempDir, 'snapshot.tar.gz')). During the decrypt and extract phases, the decrypted backup contents (including credentials.json, .env with API tokens, agent config, memory data) are temporarily readable by any user on the system. Fix: import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js and pass mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET to mkdirSync at line 151. Also apply the same fix to extractDir at line 196 which holds the extracted files.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/restore.ts lines 14-22 — confirm mkdirSync is imported but DIR_MODE_SECRET is not
Add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js at line 21
Line 151: change mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Line 196: change mkdirSync(extractDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(extractDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Create src/operate/backup/restore.test.ts — use vi.spyOn(fs, 'mkdirSync') to capture mode argument; verify first mkdirSync call (tempDir creation) passes mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET (0o700)
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: restore.ts line 151 (tempDir) creates the temp directory with mode DIR_MODE_SECRET — stat confirms no group/other read bits
restore.ts line 196 (extractDir) creates the extract directory with mode DIR_MODE_SECRET
DIR_MODE_SECRET is imported from config/defaults.js in restore.ts
restore.test.ts exists and the mkdirSync mode test passes
